channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: PGL Singapore Major 2026 to Feature Third-Place Decider; VRS Deadline Revealed
item.content:encoded:
PGL has announced that the PGL Singapore Major 2026 will feature a third-place decider for the first time in Counter-Strike major history.

The announcement also included the VRS deadline for the invites. Teams will have to amass their VRS points prior to the November 2 deadline, which will dictate who gets the spots and who misses out.

PGL announces third-place decider in another Major shake-up

PGL has opted to have a third/fourth-place decider prior to the Singapore Major’s Grand Final, creating a final chance for teams to end their major on a positive note and earn some VRS points, which will help them into the next season.

The third-place team will presumably get a greater share of the $1,250,000 prize pool than the fourth-placed team, though prize money distribution is not yet known.

The announcement is the last in a series of innovations to the major format. Both the StarLadder Budapest Major 2025 and the upcoming IEM Cologne Major 2026 feature alterations to the previous formula.

StarLadder became the first CS major in history to feature a Best of 5 Grand Final, bringing Counter-Strike’s event in line with other top tournaments in the space.

The IEM Cologne Major 2026 will be the first major to remove Best of 1 series entirely. Bo1s have heavily featured at majors in the past, most recently utilized for non-elimination/promotion Swiss stages matches.

VRS deadline set for second major of the year

The November 2 VRS deadline will be the most significant aspect of PGL’s statement for rosters and organizations.

The relatively late deadline gives teams plenty of time to plan their calendars before the final push for VRS points.

Tournament organizers will also be eyeing the deadline as a prime opportunity to fit more events before the deadline. While Tier 1 tournaments have to announce dates well in advance, local LANs have more flexibility, allowing them to target dates.

The effect of local LANs on the VRS was never clearer than the race to secure IEM Cologne Major invites, where events such as DracuLan, Parken Challenger, and HLC Belgrade Pro were attended by Counter-Strike’s top teams as they sought major qualification.

The last Tier 1 tournament before the cutoff will be PGL Masters Bucharest from October 24-31, but history has shown that it may be at these smaller local LANs that the real drama really plays out.

Do you prefer a third/fourth-place decider to show matches? Discuss that on our Discord!

For more news, see who made it through the IEM Cologne Major 2026 qualification gauntlet.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/pgl-major-third-place-vrs-deadline/

item.title: OG bench Nikko; Sign TI winner on Loan
item.content:encoded:
OG has announced the removal of Nikko ‘Nikko’ Bilocura from its active Dota 2 roster in the organization’s first roster move since its pivot to Southeast Asia.

OG announced that former Aurora Offlaner, Alexander ‘TORONTOTOKYO’ Khertek, will take Nikko’s place in the roster. The TI 2021 winner was removed from Aurora in January.

OG remove first piece since region change

Nikko’s removal signals the roster’s first change since its switch to the SEA region. The experiment thus far has been a competitive failure.

While the Filipino roster undoubtedly has brought new fans and energy to the organization, the roster has declined in form after a promising start in Tier 1 competition.

The roster looked in contention at times at PGL Wallachia Season 6, BLAST Slam V, and DreamLeague Season 27, where they finished 5th-6th.

BLAST Slam IV, a chaotic tournament in its own right, represented the high-water mark of Nikko in the roster, with a 3rd/4th place finish.

But poor finishes at DreamLeague Season 28, PGL Wallachia Season 7, and ESL Birmingham took fans’ hopes away, with Tier 1 now looking like an insurmountable wall for the team.

OG has now looked to update its roster, with the Offlane position as the org’s number one target to turn their fortunes around.

OG stated, “We’ll forever be grateful for his presence as one of the founding members of our SEA chapter. Nikko’s spirit and camaraderie were a blessing during his time with us, and we wish him well in whatever comes next.”

TORONTOTOKYO joins OG on loan

Changes in the Dota 2 7.41 patch gave Offlaners a bit of room to breathe due to changes in the lane equilibrium. Unfortunately, Nikko will not have time to benefit from it with TORONTOTOKYO now in the midlane position.

The Russian joins the roster after being benched in January as Aurora went through their own mini-rebuild. The TI 2021 winner brings more Tier 1 experience to the team, and should bring more Offlane stability, but may also cause issues.

The question of what happens to team synergy and in-game communications is raised, considering the previously fully national Filipino team now has a Russian member.

Coming from a smaller region, the question of whether the organization is going to slowly move back to EU is raised by the choice, but we’ll have to see how OG perform in the server with this one confirmed change.

Fans will have to wait to see TORONTOTOKYO in action as OG will miss the upcoming PGL Wallachia Season 8 due to Visa issues.

Is this the right direction for OG? Discuss that on our Discord!
For more news, see how Overwatch’s new hero was unveiled by Blizzard.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 9, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 9, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Found on the waterfront
  • Hint 2: Home for boats
  • Hint 3: Small port for pleasure instead of trade

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Ferry
  • Bay
  • Quay
  • Boat
  • Waterfront
  • Harbor
  • Wharf
  • Pier
  • Dock

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 9, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Marina

A Marina is a small port that is used for pleasure rather than trade, often with hotels, restaurants, and bars.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on April 10.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • Wordle
  • NYT Connections

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Overwatch’s New Hero Unveiled by Blizzard with Confusing Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Blizzard has lifted the lid on Sierra, the 51st Hero to hit Overwatch’s ecosystem, debuting the new character with a stylish cinematic that has left some fans confused. That’s mainly because the trailer seems to better showcase other, existing Heroes, rather than Sierra, a powerful new DPS (we assume).

Sierra’s origins haven’t been explained outright, but eagle-eyed fans have linked the character’s design to a 2021 novel dubbed Overwatch: Deadlock Rebels. In that book, there was a character named Frankie, a hacker, and the description of that model bears a striking resemblance to Sierra, revealed in an all-new trailer.

Sierra’s Trailer Reveals Weakness to Emre and Freja

In Sierra’s new trailer, released by Overwatch to unveil the new Hero, we see the stage set with Sierra trying to stop Talon from obtaining a mysterious, unnamed weapon. She clashes with Emre, outmaneuvering him off the bat but being quickly subdued by his Override Protocol.

Before long, Freja turns up and restrains Sierra further, pinning the new Hero down and allowing for an escape. The spotlight in Sierra’s trailer is strangely stolen by Emre, who seems to effortlessly overpower Sierra, but it at least gives us an idea of her abilities.

She seems to wield a hitscan weapon, a plasma rifle that fires energy bolts. The trailer almost gives away a secondary ability for the rifle, which looks like some kind of high-energy, precise dart mechanic.

In one segment, the weapon seems to have a homing ability, which would be a rare move for Blizzard regarding a DPS Hero.

In terms of equipment, Sierra is revealed to have a bird-like drone mounted on her arm, which assists her in traversal and tracking targets at long range. This is implied as central to Sierra’s role and makes her a mobile, capable competitor.

Are you looking forward to playing as Sierra in Overwatch? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the competitive shakeup coming to the COD League

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Huge Call of Duty Meta Shakeup Replaces M15 and Dravec
item.content:encoded:
The Call of Duty League 2026 season is fast pushing towards a solid resolution. We’ve had a stunning season thus far, which has seen the arrival of a hyper-popular Major in the United Kingdom, one that was so energetic it has all but sealed the deal for future events. Next, we’ve got Major 3, which will be held in Paris and promises to be the biggest Call of Duty tournament ever, in terms of fan engagement.

Recently, a drastic competitive shakeup was revealed, with the game’s professional players opting to GA both the M15 and the Dravec, the go-to AR and SMG weapons of choice for Black Ops 7’s meta. Following major changes to both weapons, they’ve been deemed unviable and grounds for replacement in the meta.

CDL Loadouts Are About to Change

The Call of Duty League’s slate of GAs has reportedly changed dramatically, with players opting to remove the M15 AR and Dravec SMG from the pool. This follows recent amendments to both weapons that have brought about a hit on their range capabilities, amongst other things.

In recent days, the game’s best players were bagging on the weapons, adding further fuel to the GA fire:

In response, a GA has reportedly been set up, with the duo of weapons being replaced with the MPC, KOGOT, and VOYAK. The VOYAK KT-3 and KOGOT-7 are both ‘DLC weapons’ that were introduced in recent season updates, which is relatively unconventional where meta weapons are concerned, but the MPC has been a touted alternative to the Dravec since day one.

Some of Call of Duty’s most prolific players have been spotted on social media discussing the new GA and proposed weapon switches:

Presently, the Call of Duty League is on a mid-Major hiatus and will return on Friday, April 17. That break follows the dominating performance of FaZe Vegas at Major 2 in the UK, a run that saw the team decimate OpTic Texas in the grand final to secure the trophy.

Do you agree with the impending meta change, or would you rather keep the M15 and Dravec arrangement? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the world’s first VR esports league

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: NYT Strands #767 Hints and Answers for Today, April 9, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #767 for Thursday, April 9, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, April 9, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #767 are all terms related to artistry and painting.

General Strands Hints For April 9

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Sensodyne repairs this in teeth.
  • Hint 2: Springtime colors are called this.
  • Hint 3: Not small or large.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #767 for April 9, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for April 9, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • WATER
  • RARE
  • SERF
  • COME
  • LETS

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #767 On April 9, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • FRESCO
  • ACRYLIC
  • TEMPERA
  • ENAMEL
  • PASTEL
  • WATERCOLOR
  • SPANAGRAM: MEDIUM

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #767?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on April 10 will be Strands Game #768.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
